english: W. to lie in winter-sleep; _o-brgyál-te ('o brgyal te) very much exhausted, v. _o ('o). - 2. to howl, of the fox. Sch.
Lex.; Cs.: 'the marrow in the back-bone'.

cf. rgyud (rgyud), Ssk. परम्पर fa-mily (gens), lineage; relations, ances-tors, descendants, offspring, mi-brgyúd (mi brgyud) 1. = brgyud (brgyud), dei mi-brgyúd yín-pa (de'i mi brgyud yin pa) being of his family Glr. 2. people, nation, bód-kyi mi-brgyúd (bod kyi mi brgyud) the Tibetan nation. 3. the human race, mankind Cs.; rigs-brgyúd (rigs brgyud), resp. gduṅ-brgyúd (gdung brgyud) family; issue, progeny, rigs-brgyúd pél-bar _gyur (rigs brgyud phel bar 'gyur) there will be a numerous offspring; bla-rgyúd (bla rgyud) succession or descent of Lamas Cs. - *mig ná-ce gyúd-la yod* (mig na ce rgyud la yod) W. diseases of the eye frequently occur in that family; *dé-ne gyud mi čad yin* (de ne rgyud mi chad yin) W. then the race will not die out; *spél-gyud-la bór-če* (spel rgyud la bor ce) W. to set apart cattle for breeding; brgyúd-nas brgyúd-du (brgyud nas brgyud du) from generation to generation Cs.; bu tsa brgyúd-du bdeo (bu tsha brgyud du bde'o) he is blessed even to his children and children's children Dzl. Comp. brgyud-brgyúgs (brgyud brgyugs) a continuous succession Sch. - brgyúd-čan (brgyud can) like his progenitors Cs. - brgyúd-pa (brgyud pa) 1. belonging to a race or family. 2. v. rgyud (rgyud) and rgyúd-pa (rgyud pa). - brgyud-méd (brgyud med) degenerate Cs., cf. brgyúd-čan (brgyud can). - brgyúd-ma (brgyud ma) 1. Cs. = brgyúd-čan (brgyud can). 2. W. fruitful, fertile. 3. brgyud-ma-brgyab (brgyud ma brgyab) Lex. w.e. - brgyud-dzin (brgyud 'dzin) a first-born male, heir and successor.
is acknowledged by Lexx., but evidently an incorrect form for bgrad (bgrad).
words beginning thus will for the greater part be found under sg... (sg...).
v. gégs-pa (gegs pa) and sgóg-pa (sgog pa).

(Lexx. = dṅos-gži (dngos gzhi), मुख?) point of time, moment, instant, conjuncture, lo-gsar-bsgáṅ-gi lhágs-ma (lo gsar bsgang gi lhags ma) a chilling gale on newyear s day Mil.; esp. the proper time or season for doing a thing, byá-bai bsgaṅ (bya ba'i bsgang); _bri-bai ('bri ba'i), zá-bai bsgaṅ (za ba'i bsgang) the time for writing, eating. (A different word from sgaṅ (sgang)).

1. v. sgó-ba (sgo ba). - 2. pf. bsgos (bsgos), vb. a. to gó-ba (go ba), to soil, stain, defile, lit. and fig., *kyon-ghyī ma gō* (kyon ghyi ma go) C. he was not tainted with any spot or blemish, nothing could be laid to his charge; to infect with disease; rarely in a good sense: dri sna-tsogs-kyis legs-par bsgos-pa (dri sna tshogs kyis legs par bsgos pa) Stg. well anointed with salves and perfumes.
1. to enumerate, count up (?) Cs. - 2. to cause to grow cold Lexx.

1. the letter ṅ (ng), sounded as a nasal guttural, the English ng in singing, in the Tibetan language often the initial letter of a word. - 2. as numerical figure: 4. - 3. as numeral adjective = lṅá-bču (lnga bcu), in the numbers 51-59.

pers. pron., first person sing. and pl. I, we, the usual word in familiar speech; ṅai (nga'i) my, our; mine, ours; ṅa mi rgan (nga mi rgan) old man that I am Mil.; ṅa rgyál-po sroṅ-btsan-sgám-po daṅ (nga rgyal po srong btsan sgam po dang) with me, king Srongtsangampo Glr.; blá-ma ṅa (bla ma nga) I, the Lama Mil.; de mi rgan ṅai ká-la nyon (de mi rgan nga'i ka la nyon) listen to my word as that of an old man Mil.; ṅai _di (nga'i 'di) this my (doing) Glr.; ṅai rje-btsún (nga'i rje btsun) my honoured masters! Mil.; ṅai (nga'i)
